sourceMovie.title: Blue Valentine
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Blue Valentine works as a drama movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The drama and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Silver Linings Playbook
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Blue Valentine clicked for you, Silver Linings Playbook (2012) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and romance, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Illusionist
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Blue Valentine clicked for you, The Illusionist (2006) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and romance, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Blue Valentine?
faq.answer: Try Silver Linings Playbook, The Illusionist, Step Up—each shares DNA with Blue Valentine while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Blue Valentine?
faq.answer: Blue Valentine (2010) is best described as Drama, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Blue Valentine worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
